agentFile.bodyIs: structured-fields (a 'customModes' array of mode objects; not free-form markdown). YAML is preferred for readability, comments, and multi-line strings; JSON legacy. .roomodes is project-scoped and overrides global custom_modes.yaml/json entirely for a matching slug.

slug.description: Unique internal identifier for the mode; used in directory/file names such as .roo/rules-{slug}/.

name.allowed: any string (spaces and capitalization allowed; may include emoji)
name.description: Display name shown in the Roo Code mode selector UI.

roleDefinition.allowed: free text; multi-line supported (YAML | literal or > folded blocks)
roleDefinition.description: Defines the core identity and expertise of the mode; placed at the very start of the system prompt.

description.description: Short, user-friendly summary shown below the mode name in the selector.

whenToUse.description: Guidance for Roo's automated/orchestration mode-selection decisions; not displayed in the UI.

customInstructions.description: Specific behavioral guidelines appended near the end of the system prompt for this mode.

groups.allowed: elements from: 'read', 'edit', 'browser', 'command', 'mcp'. The 'edit' element may instead be a two-element tuple ["edit", { fileRegex, description }] to restrict editable files.
groups.description: Allowed tool groups (toolsets) and file-access permissions for the mode. The edit group can carry a fileRegex restriction object with fields: fileRegex (regex string, matched against full relative path, case-sensitive; single-backslash escape in YAML, double-backslash in JSON) and description (optional human label).

source.description: Internal field indicating mode origin; auto-added by Roo Code and should not be set manually.

model.description: unverified - per-mode model is assigned via API Configuration Profiles in the Settings/Prompts UI (with sticky per-mode memory), not via a documented field inside the .roomodes/custom_modes.yaml mode object.

iconName.description: unverified - not documented in the official custom-modes field list.

bashInjection.description: No documented inline shell-command execution/injection syntax (e.g. no !`cmd` style) inside mode definitions, rules, or command files. Command execution happens only at runtime via the 'command' tool group, not via templating tokens in the definition files.

fileInjection.description: unverified - no documented file-embed/include token (e.g. @file) for inlining file contents into a mode definition, rule, or slash command file. Roo loads whole rule directories rather than embedding files via a token.

argumentTokens.description: Roo commands accept free-text arguments typed after /name (described via the argument-hint frontmatter); no $-substitution tokens are documented.

toolReference.description: Tool access is declared, not referenced inline. A mode's 'groups' array enables tool groups; the 'edit' group may be restricted by a fileRegex object (regex matched against the full relative path from workspace root, case-sensitive by default).

mcpReference.description: MCP usage is gated per mode by including 'mcp' in the mode's groups array. MCP servers are configured separately in an mcpServers object (project .roo/mcp.json and global settings) with command/args/env or url/headers plus alwaysAllow/disabled. Exact Roo MCP field list is unverified (not re-fetched from the official MCP page).

variableTemplating.description: unverified - no documented general variable-templating/interpolation syntax (e.g. {{var}}) within mode definitions or rules files.

agentMention.description: A custom slash command may target/switch to a mode via its frontmatter 'mode' field (a mode slug, e.g. 'code', 'architect'). There is no documented inline @agent mention token for invoking another mode mid-prompt.

generalRules: AGENTS.md or AGENT.md (workspace root, auto-loaded, can be disabled)
modeSpecificRules.loadingOrderNote: Loads from ALL applicable dirs (global ~/.roo/ + workspace .roo/); workspace overrides global on conflict. Files read recursively, sorted by basename case-insensitively.
